final_prompt.prompt:
Create an astronomical science visualization editorial photo of the Milky Way galactic center region, rendered in the imaging style of the Hubble Space Telescope and James Webb Space Telescope.

Style/medium: photorealistic astronomical science visualization, composite imaging style blending Hubble ACS/WFC3 deep-field color palette with JWST NIRCam infrared false-color processing; inspired by NASA/ESA official release aesthetics
Composition: wide-field deep-space frame centered on the supermassive black hole, surrounded by layered cosmic structures extending to frame edges; no single vanishing point — expansive, immersive, symmetrical gravitational geometry at center
Subject: supermassive black hole at image center with a glowing accretion disk emitting intense orange-red and white radiation; gravitational lensing effect bending background starlight into a perfect Einstein ring; surrounding stellar field densely packed with hundreds of millions of stars ranging from blue-white young hot stars to orange-red aged giant stars; several dramatic nebula gas pillars extending from frame edges in emission nebula colors of red, orange, and purple; two colliding and merging galaxies visible in the far background with spectacular tidal streams
Scene/backdrop: deep-space black as dominant background, populated with countless star points and colorful nebulae; galactic core environment with high stellar density and interstellar gas structures
Lighting/mood: self-luminous cosmic sources — accretion disk as primary light, diffuse nebula glow as secondary fill; no external light source; mood is grand, awe-inspiring, scientifically grounded
Color palette: deep space black as base; orange-red and white as accretion disk anchors; blue-white and orange-red as stellar population contrast; red, orange, purple as nebula accent tones; tidal stream galaxies in soft blue-white
Materials/textures: gaseous nebula pillar texture with wispy filament detail; star surfaces rendered as point sources with diffraction spikes consistent with space telescope optics; accretion disk with smooth gradient luminosity falloff
Constraints: scientifically accurate depiction — Einstein ring geometry must be physically plausible; accretion disk orientation must be consistent with black hole shadow; tidal streams must show realistic arc morphology
Avoid: cartoon or fantasy space art; lens flare from artificial light sources; over-saturated neon colors inconsistent with real astronomical imaging; watermark; stuffer quality words
